title: Wrong results in scalar subquery with aggregation in out query target list
description:
### What happens?

Wrong results in outer-level aggregation of sublinks

### To Reproduce

```
create table tenk1(
 unique1 int4,
 unique2 int4,
 two int4,
 four int4,
 ten int4,
 twenty int4,
 hundred int4,
 thousand int4);

insert into tenk1 values (1,1,1,1,1,1,1,1);
insert into tenk1 values (2,2,2,2,2,2,2,2);

select
  (select max((select i.unique2 from tenk1 i where i.unique1 = o.unique1)))
from tenk1 o;
```
```
┌───────┐
│   x   │
│ int32 │
├───────┤
│     1 │
│     2 │
└───────┘
```

```
select                              
  (select max((select i.unique2 from tenk1 i where i.unique1 = o.unique1)))
from tenk1 o;
```
```
 max 
-----
   2
(1 row)
```

### OS:

Mac OS

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.0

### DuckDB Client:

Cli

_No response_

poc: CREATE TABLE tenk1 (unique1 INT, unique2 INT, two INT, four INT, ten INT, twenty INT, hundred INT, thousand INT);
SELECT (SELECT MAX((SELECT i.unique2 FROM tenk1 i WHERE i.unique1 = o.unique1))) FROM tenk1 o;
SELECT (SELECT MAX(unique2) FROM tenk1 i WHERE i.unique1 = o.unique1) FROM tenk1 o;
key_bug_features: Outer-level aggregation in scalar subquery with aggregation
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value VARCHAR, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT (SELECT MAX((SELECT t.amount FROM test_table t WHERE t.id = o.id))) FROM test_table o;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL) AS null_count, (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM test_table) AS avg_amount;
